review_reason: Phase 5 density proof blocked: staged_timeline_densely_ordered requires proving canonical model density frame condition (CanonicalR M M' implies exists W with CanonicalR M W and CanonicalR W M'), which requires controlling GContent of Lindenbaum witnesses - fundamentally hard under irreflexive semantics. All other Phase 5 properties proven (countable, nonempty, NoMaxOrder, NoMinOrder). Phases 6-8 blocked by density dependency.

artifacts.summary: Core staged timeline types, ordering, monotonicity, union
Forward/backward witness construction and CanonicalR properties
Distinguishing formulas, Case A separation, density intermediates
Full staged execution with even/odd stages, linearity, monotonicity
Cantor prerequisites: countable, nonempty, NoMaxOrder, NoMinOrder proven; DenselyOrdered BLOCKED
Phase 5 partial: 4/5 Cantor prerequisites proven, density blocked

partial_progress.details: Phase 5 partially complete: 4/5 Cantor prerequisites proven sorry-free. DenselyOrdered blocked by fundamental difficulty in controlling GContent of Lindenbaum witnesses under irreflexive semantics. Same blocker as DenseQuotient.lean in Boneyard (research-034 Finding 8-11). Phases 6-8 depend on density.

completion_data.completion_summary: Fixed staged_has_past sorry and proved 4 of 5 Cantor prerequisites (countable, nonempty, NoMaxOrder, NoMinOrder). DenselyOrdered is blocked by canonical model density frame condition under irreflexive semantics.

errors.message: staged_timeline_densely_ordered requires proving canonical model density frame condition (forall M M', CanonicalR M M' -> exists W, CanonicalR M W /\ CanonicalR W M'). Under irreflexive semantics, the Lindenbaum witness W's GContent cannot be controlled to ensure CanonicalR W M'. This is the same fundamental difficulty identified in research-034 (Findings 8-11) and confirmed by DenseQuotient.lean sorries in Boneyard.
errors.recommendation: Options: (1) Research new proof technique for density under irreflexive semantics (may require axiom-level innovation). (2) Consider whether a weaker ordering property suffices for the D construction. (3) Consider alternative to Cantor isomorphism that doesn't require DenselyOrdered.
